businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue reached $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD has shifted to a $99/month subscription model, while Cybercab production and Roadster reveal are both set for April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy generation and storage (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: S&P 500 avg ~23x, Auto sector avg ~12x. Extreme premium persists even with RSI 33 oversold

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS $2.08. Over 4x the growth stock average of 30-40x. Prices in robotaxi and energy moonshot scenarios

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: 23-analyst consensus. +8% vs Q1 2025 but -13% vs Q4 2025. Report expected early next week

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Weekend, no change at $361.83. YTD -20%. NFP and Q1 deliveries next week will set direction

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. CCI -151.82, Williams %R -95.73. Technical bounce probability rising

growthDrivers.title: Tesla captures 50%+ EV market share amid 28% industry slump (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: While the overall EV market cratered 28%, Tesla grew its share above 50%. Brand dominance and Supercharger infrastructure prove strongest during downturns

growthDrivers.title: SpaceX IPO rumors fuel Tesla ecosystem revaluation (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: SpaceX IPO preparation rumors are spreading, potentially catalyzing a revaluation of the broader Musk ecosystem for Tesla investors

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot video released, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla revealed Optimus3 gear reducers and precision hand design. Targeting 1M unit production rate by end of 2026. Trefis values the robot business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster reveal late April
growthDrivers.description: Steerless fully autonomous Cybercab robotaxi enters production alongside next-gen Roadster reveal, two major catalysts concentrated in April

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI chip factory, Wedbush calls it 'game changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reaffirmed $600 target, calling Terafab a game changer. AI5 chip small-scale production starts late 2026, full production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA escalates FSD investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA upgraded its probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Trefis warns this could undermine the '$1T AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market 50%+ share, FSD 8.4B miles of driving data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4B real-world FSD miles. Scale of autonomous driving data that competitors cannot replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others use Tesla's infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand awareness and passionate fan base generate demand with zero marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price competitiveness advantage rapidly closing the gap

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Las Vegas. Austin and Miami trials started. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead in autonomous commercialization. 200K+ paid rides/week. Expanding robotaxi service

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Ioniq, EV6 expanding global EV share. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: CleanTechnica: 'Down 20% in 6 months, are people giving up on Tesla robotaxis?'
recentNews.significance: Growing robotaxi skepticism as stock drops 20% over 6 months. Seeking Alpha maintains Strong Sell, calling the robotaxi story 'a myth'

recentNews.headline: Musk challenges Twitter fraud verdict, claims jury's '$4.20 joke' proves bias
recentNews.significance: Musk's legal team argues a juror's $4.20 annotation mocked him, seeking a new trial. Legal risk dragging on and distracting management

recentNews.headline: Citigroup cuts US equity exposure; Dow enters correction territory on Hormuz fears
recentNews.significance: Dow officially entered correction (-10% from peak). Strait of Hormuz blockade fears drove oil surge, pressuring the broader market

recentNews.headline: Tesla captures 50%+ EV market share in brutal 28% EV sales slump
recentNews.significance: Tesla expanded share even as the EV market collapsed. Proving competitive strength in adversity

recentNews.headline: Q1 2026 delivery consensus 365,645 vehicles published
recentNews.significance: 23-analyst average. +8% YoY but -13% QoQ. Actual delivery numbers expected early next week

analystOpinions.notableComment: 43 analysts: 15 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 17 Hold, 9 Strong Sell. Wedbush $600 high, HSBC $131 low. Seeking Alpha maintains Strong Sell with 'robotaxi myth' thesis

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day moving averages, downtrend accelerating
risks.description: Trading below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). RSI 33 near oversold but no reversal signal. 5 consecutive down days before weekend

risks.title: Extreme valuation + growing robotaxi skepticism
risks.description: TTM P/E 335x, 14.5x S&P 500 average. CleanTechnica 'giving up on robotaxis', Seeking Alpha 'robotaxi myth'. Skepticism growing as stock falls

risks.title: Next week macro risk concentration (Dow in correction)
risks.description: Dow entered correction territory. Hormuz blockade fears driving oil surge. NFP (4/3), Iran conflict, and 4/6 tariff deadline all converging

risks.title: Q1 delivery concerns + Musk legal risk
risks.description: Consensus 365,645 units, -13% QoQ. Musk seeking new trial in Twitter fraud case. Management distraction concerns

risks.title: Bull defection (Trip Chowdhry to $150 Sell)
risks.description: Former Tesla bull Trip Chowdhry flipped to Sell with $150 target. Symbolic sentiment shift

risks.title: Musk brand risk
risks.description: DOGE political activities causing European sales decline. Insider selling continues. Brand damage directly impacting demand

buyReasons.title: Market share expansion in EV downturn proves competitive moat
buyReasons.rationale: 50%+ share despite 28% EV market crash. Strongest get stronger in downturns. Positioned for maximum benefit when market recovers

buyReasons.title: April catalyst concentration (Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Netherlands FSD approval, Q1 earnings (4/21) all in April. Potential for short-term momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: RSI 33, technical oversold territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI approaching 30 oversold threshold. -27% from 52-week high. Technical bounce probability rising, though trend reversal confirmation needed

buyReasons.title: SpaceX IPO + Optimus robot as long-term rerating catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: SpaceX IPO rumors and Optimus3 production start could justify Tesla's 'beyond automaker' valuation as long-term catalysts

overallOpinion: Next week is Tesla's inflection point. The Dow has entered correction territory, and NFP (4/3) and Q1 deliveries (365K consensus) arrive simultaneously. Strait of Hormuz blockade fears are driving oil higher, rapidly worsening the macro backdrop

Robotaxi skepticism is growing. CleanTechnica asked 'are people giving up on robotaxis?' and Seeking Alpha called the story 'a myth' with Strong Sell. Six months of -20% decline is wearing on investor patience
Yet 50%+ EV market share in a 28% crash is real proof of competitive advantage. April brings Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, and FSD Europe expansion as concentrated catalysts
$361.83, RSI 33 oversold. Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). Macro deterioration and robotaxi skepticism create near-term headwinds. Hold, watch through 4/21 earnings

investmentVerdict.summary: RSI 33 oversold and April catalyst concentration support a bounce case, but Dow correction, Hormuz crisis, and spreading robotaxi skepticism weigh heavily. Q1 deliveries and NFP next week are critical. Hold is prudent; new positions safer below $340 support

threads.hook: TSLA: Robotaxi skepticism amid oversold conditions, Q1 deliveries will decide
threads.text:
$TSLA $361.83 (weekend, Friday close)

'Are people giving up on robotaxis?' Six months of -20% has fueled skepticism. The Dow entered correction and Hormuz fears are driving oil higher.

But 50%+ EV share in a 28% crash proves real competitive strength. April brings Cybercab, Roadster, and FSD Europe.

RSI 33 oversold. Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). Hold. Watch Q1 deliveries next week.

witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83 (weekend)

Seeking Alpha: 'Robotaxis are a myth'. Tesla: 'Cybercab production starts April'.

We'll start finding out next week. -20% in 6 months with the Dow in correction.

thread:
EV market crashed 28% but Tesla took 50%+ share. Cars sell fine, but the robotaxi story is the problem.

Add Hormuz and oil surge. Buffet of bad news

If it's real, the myth becomes reality.

Hold. April is judgment month

witty.hook: Robotaxis: myth or reality? April will deliver the verdict
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83 (weekend)

Seeking Alpha calls robotaxis 'a myth'. Tesla starts Cybercab production in April.

-20% in 6 months, Dow in correction. EV market crashed 28% but Tesla grabbed 50%+ share. Ironic.

Hormuz fears, oil surge, RSI 33 but P/E 335x. Oversold yet expensive at the same time.

Hold. April is Tesla's moment of truth.

empathy.hook: TSLA shareholders, robotaxi skepticism headlines are piling on. But April is right around the corner
empathy.text:
$TSLA $361.83. -20% in 6 months. 'Giving up on robotaxis' headlines now.

Dow correction, Hormuz fears, oil surge. You're exhausted. RSI 33 shows that fatigue in numbers.

But 50%+ EV share in a crash is real strength. Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe all coming in April.

Q1 deliveries and NFP next week will set direction. Target $408 (+12.8%). Stay patient. Hold.
